variants.rationale: Leads with the concrete offer and roast dating transparency in nine words — no filler, exactly what Andrei scans for before deleting.

variants.text: What's in the Gelana this harvest?
variants.rationale: Names a specific micro-lot and frames it as a harvest-level question, triggering the same itch Andrei gets reading Sprudge origin reports.

variants.text: You know what to look for. Good.
variants.rationale: Peer-to-peer framing that skips the brand introduction and positions Sebastian as a roaster talking to someone already fluent in the language.

variants.text: We don't roast for café menus
variants.rationale: A quietly confident declaration of focus that signals Coffra's micro-lot positioning without self-aggrandisement, earning trust from someone allergic to marketing posture.

variants.text: Ever cupped a washed Guji blind?
variants.rationale: Uses technical vocabulary naturally and poses a low-pressure sensory challenge that speaks directly to Andrei's V60-and-Comandante weekend ritual.
